questions.q: As of March 16, 2026, a new cap on 'Non-Sufficient Funds' (NSF) fees officially took effect in Canada. What is the maximum amount a bank can now charge a consumer for an NSF fee?
Which iconic Canadian landmark is being lit green every hour for 15 minutes starting at 8:00 PM on March 17, 2026, as part of a global St. Patrick's Day tourism initiative?
The Canadian government announced a $200 million investment on March 17, 2026, to support a new spaceport located in which province?

On March 17, 2026, reports emerged that the federal government is planning to achieve $60 billion in savings by reducing the size of the public service. Which agency is projected to see its workforce peak this year due to the 2026 Census?
Which major tech company completed its $11 billion acquisition of the data streaming platform Confluent on March 17, 2026, to bolster its 'real-time' AI capabilities?
